title: NVR Recording Segment Gap Detection vs Expected FPS
dataSources: `index=physical` `sourcetype=nvr:recording_health` JSON or CSV from Milestone, Genetec, or Hikvision SDK exports with `segment_start_epoch`, `segment_end_epoch`, `camera_id`
app: Custom scripted export via NVR REST API + HEC, or VMS SIEM connector
spl:
index=physical sourcetype=nvr:recording_health camera_id=*
| sort 0 camera_id segment_start_epoch
| streamstats window=1 global=f last(segment_end_epoch) as prev_end by camera_id
| eval gap_sec=segment_start_epoch-prev_end
| where isnotnull(prev_end) AND gap_sec > 15
| table camera_id, prev_end, segment_start_epoch, gap_sec
| sort - gap_sec
description: Regulators and prosecutors ask whether video exists for a full timeline. Comparing contiguous recording segments per camera surfaces silent gaps longer than motion-mask explanations—often disk stalls, RAID degradation, or timezone skew after DST.
value: Produces defensible proof that continuous recording obligations are met, or flags gaps while footage might still be recovered from buffer.
implementation: Export segment metadata every 5 minutes; do not index full video in Splunk. Ensure epoch times are UTC. `gap_sec > 15` matches many jurisdictions' tolerance—tighten for high-security sites. Handle multiplexed streams by `camera_id`+`stream` key. Join with storage latency metrics.
visualization: Gantt-style (custom viz) or Table (gaps), Line chart (gap count per site).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• API rate limits respected on NVR.
• Legal team sets acceptable gap threshold.

Step 1 — Validate against VMS built-in gap report.

Step 2 — DST simulation in lab.

Step 3 — Chain of custody SOP for exports.

Step 4 — Optional `fillnull` for missing export windows.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Sensor anomalies during cleaning, calibration, or planned environmental changes.
